full_name: PFC 5.0 Flat-Joint Contact Model Reference (from pfc5help)
description: Complete Flat-Joint model syntax, properties, methods, and callback events for PFC 5.0, extracted from pfc5help/common/contactmodel/flatjoint/doc/manual/cmflatjoint.html

sections.heading: Overview
sections.content:
The flat-joint model (flatjoint) simulates the behavior of an interface between two notional surfaces, each connected rigidly to a piece. The interface is discretized into elements, each being either bonded or unbonded. Bonded elements are linear elastic until strength is exceeded; unbonded elements are linear elastic and frictional with Coulomb slip limit. The model can be installed at both ball-ball and ball-facet contacts.

Better UCS/TS ratio (~10-20) than linearpbond (~5), suitable for bonded-particle rock models.

sections.heading: Installation Commands
sections.content:
Install on all existing contacts:
  contact model flatjoint
  contact property fj_track on

Bond ball-ball contacts within gap distance:
  contact method bond gap <f> range contact type ball-ball
  Example: contact method bond gap 2.0e-4 range contact type ball-ball

Set stiffness via deformability method:
  contact method deform emod <E> krat <R>
  Note: actual example rocktest/flat_joint.p3dat uses 'krat' (short form).
  The keyword in the method table is 'kratio'; both forms work.

Set default for future contacts via CMAT:
  cmat default model flatjoint ...
      property fj_track on ...
      method deform emod <E> krat <R> ...
      type ball-ball

sections.heading: Properties (Modifiable)
sections.content:
fj_ten    FLT  [0,∞)  default=0.0  -- Tensile strength [stress]
fj_coh    FLT  [0,∞)  default=0.0  -- Cohesion [stress]
fj_fa     FLT  [0,90) default=0.0  -- Friction angle [degrees]
fj_fric   FLT  [0,∞)  default=0.0  -- Friction coefficient
fj_kn     FLT  [0,∞)  default=0.0  -- Normal stiffness [stress/disp.]
fj_ks     FLT  [0,∞)  default=0.0  -- Shear stiffness [stress/disp.]
fj_track  BOOL {true,false} default=false -- Tracking state for contact plotting
fj_elem   INT  [1,total] default=1 -- Element index for element-based data access

sections.heading: Properties (Read-only / Install-time)
sections.content:
fj_nr     INT  [1,∞)  default=2  -- Elements in radial dir. (total in 2D)
fj_nal    INT  [3,∞)  default=4  -- Elements in circumferential dir. (3D only)
fj_rmul   FLT  (0,∞)  default=1.0 -- Radius multiplier
fj_gap0   FLT  [0,∞)  default=0.0 -- Initial surface gap
fj_emod   FLT  [0,∞)  default=0.0 -- Effective modulus (set via method, then read-only)
fj_kratio FLT  [0,∞)  default=0.0 -- Normal-to-shear stiffness ratio (set via method, then read-only)
fj_radius FLT  read-only -- Flat-joint radius (set via fj_rmul * R)
fj_gap    FLT  read-only -- Surface gap
fj_slip   BOOL read-only -- Slip state of element
fj_state  INT  {0,1,2,3} -- Bond state: 0=unbonded, 1=...(see note)
fj_force  VEC  read-only -- Interface force
fj_moment VEC  read-only -- Interface moment
fj_mtype  INT  {1,2,3,4} -- Initial microstructural type (install-time, read-only)

NOTE: fj_nr, fj_nal, fj_rmul, fj_gap0, fj_mtype are set at first cycle after
installation and CANNOT be modified afterwards.

sections.heading: Methods
sections.content:
1. deformability -- Set stiffness via effective modulus and kratio:
     contact method deform emod <E> kratio <R>
     Sets: kn = E / (R1+R2)  and  ks = kn / kratio

2. bond -- Bond element(s):
     contact method bond                    -- Bond all elements (if overlap)
     contact method bond element <i>        -- Bond specific element
     contact method bond gap <f1> [<f2>]    -- Bond if gap within interval
     Example: contact method bond gap 2.0e-4 range contact type ball-ball

3. unbond -- Unbond element(s):
     contact method unbond
     contact method unbond element <i>
     contact method unbond gap <f1> [<f2>]

Note: The method command is issued via the generic 'contact method' syntax:
  contact method bond|deform|unbond ...

sections.heading: Callback Events
sections.content:
bond_break -- Triggered when a bonded element fails (tension or shear):
    entries(1) = C_PNT  -- Contact address (contact pointer)
    entries(2) = INT    -- Element number (e)
    entries(3) = INT    -- Break mode: 1=tension, 2=shear

slip_change -- Triggered when slip state of element changes:
    entries(1) = C_PNT  -- Contact address
    entries(2) = INT    -- Element number (e)
    entries(3) = INT    -- Slip change mode: {0,1}

Registration (PFC5):
  set fishcall bond_break @your_callback_function

sections.heading: Energy Partitions
sections.content:
estrain -- Strain energy stored in springs (NOT accumulated)
eslip   -- Total energy dissipated by frictional slip (accumulated)

Accessible via: contact.prop(cp, 'estrain') and contact.prop(cp, 'eslip')

sections.heading: Bond State (fj_state) Values
sections.content:
0 = Unbonded (element is not bonded)
1 = Bonded (element is intact)
2 = Bond broken in tension
3 = Bond broken in shear

sections.heading: Full Working Example (from rocktest/flat_joint.p3dat)
sections.content:
; Complete Flat-Joint installation workflow for PFC 5.0:
restore unbonded

contact model flatjoint
contact property fj_track on

; bond ball-ball contacts
contact method bond gap 2.0e-4 range contact type ball-ball

; set stiffness using deformability method
contact method deform emod 1.0e9 krat 1.0

; set bond strengths
contact property fj_ten 10.0e6 fj_coh 50.0e6 fj_fa 0.0

; set ball-ball friction to non-zero value
contact property fj_fric 0.577 range contact type ball-ball

; Set new ball-ball contacts to be flatjoint (unbonded)
cmat default model flatjoint ...
    property fj_track on ...
    method deform emod 1.0e9 krat 1.0 ...
    type ball-ball

; Reset system
ball attribute displacement multiply 0.0
contact property lin_force 0.0 0.0 0.0 lin_mode 1
ball attribute contactforce multiply 0.0 contactmoment multiply 0.0
cycle 1
solve aratio 1e-5

save flatjoint

sections.heading: PFC5-Specific Notes
sections.content:
1. 'contact method' is the PFC5 command to invoke contact model methods.
   In PFC7+, the syntax changed to 'contact method' too (mostly compatible).

2. The deformability method keyword is 'kratio' in the official docs, but
   'krat' also works (verified in rocktest/flat_joint.p3dat).
   Both:  method deform emod 20e9 kratio 2.5
          method deform emod 20e9 krat 2.5

3. 'range contact type ball-ball' is REQUIRED on bond command to avoid
   attempting to bond ball-wall contacts.

4. 'fj_track on' enables contact tracking state for plotting - required
   for proper DFN fracture creation visualization.

5. Properties marked 'NO[1]' (fj_nr, fj_nal, fj_rmul, fj_gap0) are frozen
   after the first cycle following installation. Set them before cycling.

6. After restoring a saved bonded state, the flatjoint model properties
   are preserved (fj_ten, fj_coh, etc. remain as set).
